html:
Book Your Winter NYC Trip 2026: Experience the Magic of the Season

There’s something truly special about New York City in winter. The lights are brighter, the energy is electric, and the holiday spirit transforms the city into a living snow globe. For students, this is more than just a trip—it’s a chance to explore one of the world’s greatest cities during its most enchanting time of year.

With Landmark Educational Tours, book your Winter NYC Trip 2026 promises an unforgettable blend of learning, adventure, and holiday wonder. Here’s a look at the highlights that make this trip the ultimate winter getaway for student groups.

Book Your Winter NYC Trip 2026

Explore Iconic NYC Landmarks

Start your trip in the heart of Times Square, where dazzling billboards and nonstop energy welcome your group to the city that never sleeps. Then take the Staten Island Ferry for breathtaking views of the Statue of Liberty and the Manhattan skyline—perfect for those group photos!

Walk across the legendary Brooklyn Bridge, a true architectural marvel that connects Manhattan and Brooklyn. The bridge’s winter views are unforgettable, especially as the city lights sparkle against the crisp night sky.

And of course, no visit is complete without the Empire State Building Observation Deck. From the top, students can admire panoramic views of New York’s winter landscape, from Central Park’s snowy canopy to the glittering skyline below.

Dive Into Art, History, and Reflection

A visit to the 9/11 Memorial Park provides a moment of reflection and remembrance. Students can learn about the resilience of a city that rose stronger from tragedy—a meaningful stop that connects deeply with lessons in history and empathy.

Next, step inside the world-famous Metropolitan Museum of Art for a self-guided tour. With over two million works spanning 5,000 years, The Met is a perfect opportunity for students to explore art, history, and culture on a global scale—all under one iconic roof.

Celebrate the Holidays in True New York Fashion

Winter in NYC wouldn’t be complete without the city’s most festive traditions. Your group will:

  • Explore the Christmas Market at Bryant Park, filled with charming vendors, seasonal treats, and a sparkling ice rink.

  • Walk past Macy’s window displays, where each year’s elaborate designs tell magical holiday stories.

  • Attend the Today Show at Rockefeller Plaza, where students can catch a glimpse of live television production surrounded by the iconic Rockefeller Center Christmas tree.

  • Experience the legendary Christmas Spectacular starring the Rockettes at Radio City Music Hall, a performance that perfectly captures the joy and wonder of the season.

Taste, Learn, and Enjoy

Food lovers in your group will adore the Original Greenwich Village Food Tour, sampling delicious bites while exploring one of NYC’s most artistic neighborhoods. Combine this with a scenic exploration of Central Park, where students can see famous landmarks like Bethesda Terrace, Bow Bridge, and the Imagine Mosaic—all dusted in winter snow.

And of course, cap off the trip with a Broadway Show, where students can experience the magic of live theater in one of the most iconic performing arts capitals in the world.

Why Travel to NYC in Winter 2026?

Winter in New York is more than just a season—it’s an experience. The festive decorations, special events, and crisp atmosphere make it the perfect time for student travel. Educationally, the trip connects art, history, culture, and performing arts, all within one incredible city.

A Winter NYC Trip 2026 with Landmark Educational Tours is the perfect blend of holiday magic, education, and exploration. From world-famous landmarks and Broadway lights to meaningful learning experiences, this itinerary captures the very best of the Big Apple in its most enchanting season.

Ready to plan your school’s New York City winter adventure?
Contact Landmark Educational Tours today to customize your 2026 itinerary and make next winter one your students will never forget! ❄️🗽✨
